services.name: pub.date:calculateDateDifference
services.description: WmPublic. Calculates the difference between two dates and returns the result as seconds, minutes, hours, and days.

input_parameters.name: startDate
input_parameters.description: String Starting date and time.

input_parameters.name: endDate
input_parameters.description: String Ending date and time.

input_parameters.name: startDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the startDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: endDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the endDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

output_parameters.name: dateDifferenceSeconds
output_parameters.description: String The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime, truncated to the nearest whole number of seconds.

output_parameters.name: dateDifferenceMinutes
output_parameters.description: String The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime, truncated to the nearest whole number of minutes.

output_parameters.name: dateDifferenceHours
output_parameters.description: String The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime, truncated to the nearest whole number of hours.

output_parameters.name: dateDifferenceDays
output_parameters.description: String The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime, truncated to the nearest whole number of days.

usage_notes: Each output value represents the same date difference, but in a different scale. Do not add these values together. Make sure your subsequent flow steps use the correct output, depending on the scale required.

services.name: pub.date:compareDates
services.description: WmPublic. Compares two dates and returns the result as an integer.

input_parameters.name: startDate
input_parameters.description: String Starting date to compare against endDate.

input_parameters.name: endDate
input_parameters.description: String Ending date to compare against startDate.

input_parameters.name: startDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the startDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: endDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the endDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

output_parameters.name: result
output_parameters.description: String Checks whether startDate is before, the same, or after endDate.

output_parameters.name: startDate
output_parameters.description: The startDate is after the endDate.

output_parameters.name: startDate
output_parameters.description: The startDate is the same as the endDate.

output_parameters.name: startDate
output_parameters.description: The startDate is before the endDate.

usage_notes: If the formats specified in the startDatePattern and endDatePattern parameters are different, Integration Server takes the units that are not specified in the starDate and endDate values as 0.

That is, if the startDatePattern is yyyyMMdd HH:mm and the startDate is 20151030 11:11 and if the endDatePattern is yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSSand the endDate is 20151030 11:11:55:111, then the pub.date:compareDates service considers start date to be before the end date and will return the result as -1.
To calculate the difference between two dates, use the pub.date:calculateDateDifference service.

services.name: pub.date:currentNanoTime
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the current time returned by the most precise system timer, in nanoseconds.

output_parameters.name: nanoTime
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Long Current time returned by the most precise system timer, in nanoseconds.

services.name: pub.date:dateBuild

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern representing the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols. If you do not specify pattern, dateBuild returns null. If pattern contains a time zone and timezone is not specified, the default time zone of webMethods Integration Server is used.

input_parameters.name: year
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The year expressed in yyyy or yy format (for example, 01 or 2001). If you do not specify year or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current year.

input_parameters.name: month
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for January, 2 for February). If you do not specify month or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current month.

input_parameters.name: dayofmonth
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The day of the month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for the first day of the month, 2 for the second day of the month). If you do not specify dayofmonth or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current day.

input_parameters.name: timezone
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone, the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002, and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002. If locale is not specified, Integration Serveruses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The date specified by year, month, and dayofmonth, in the format of pattern.

usage_notes: The pub.date:dateBuild service creates a date String by using the supplied year, month, and dayofmonth to first create a local java.util.Date object. The local java.util.Date object uses the timezone of the machine running Integration Server. The pub.date:dateBuild service then applies the offset between the local timezone and the specified timezone. Finally, the service uses pattern to convert the java.util.Date object to the String returned in value. Due to the way in which the pub.date:dateBuild service works, if you run the pub.date:dateBuild service with the same set of inputs on two different machines, each located in a different timezone, you will get different results.

services.name: pub.date:dateTimeBuild

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern representing the format in which you want the time returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols. If you do not specify pattern, dateTimeBuild returns null. If pattern contains a time zone and the timezone parameter is not set, the time zone of Integration Server is used.

input_parameters.name: year
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The year expressed in yyyy or yy format (for example, 01 or 2001). If you do not specify year or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current year.

input_parameters.name: month
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for January, 2 for February). If you do not specify month or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current month.

input_parameters.name: dayofmonth
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The day of the month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for the first day of the month, 2 for the second day of the month). If you do not specify dayofmonth or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current day.

input_parameters.name: hour
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The hour expressed as a number based on a 24-hour clock. For example, specify 0 for midnight, 2 for 2:00 A.M., and 14 for 2:00 P.M. If you do not specify hour or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the hour value.

input_parameters.name: minute
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Minutes expressed as a number. If you do not specify minute or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the minute value.

input_parameters.name: second
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Seconds expressed as a number. If you do not specify second or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the second value.

input_parameters.name: millis
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Milliseconds expressed as a number. If you do not specify millis or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the millis value.

input_parameters.name: timezone
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone, the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002, and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Date and time in format of pattern.

usage_notes: The pub.date:dateTimeBuild service creates a date String by using the supplied year, month, dayofmonth, hour, minute, second, and millis to first create a local java.util.Date object. The local java.util.Date object uses the timezone of the machine running Integration Server. The pub.date:dateTimeBuild service then applies the offset between the local timezone and the specified timezone. Finally, the service uses pattern to convert the java.util.Date object to the String returned in value. Due to the way in which the pub.date:dateTimeBuild service works, if you run the pub.date:dateTimeBuild service with the same set of inputs on two different machines, each located in a different timezone, you will get different results.

services.name: pub.date:dateTimeFormat
services.description: WmPublic. Converts date/time (represented as a String) string from one format to another.

input_parameters.name: inString
input_parameters.description: String Date/time that you want to convert. Important: If inString contains a character in the last position, that character is interpreted as 0. This can result in an inaccurate date. For information about invalid dates, see Notes on Invalid Dates.

input_parameters.name: currentPattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern string that describes the format of inString. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: newPattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern string that describes the format in which you want inString returned. For pattern-string syntax, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the language and regional format of the input date string. For example, if the locale is en (for English), and the pattern is EEE d MMM yyyy, the input string should be Friday 23 August 2002. If the locale is fr (for French), the input string should be vendredi 23 août 2002. If the selected locale does not match the language of the input string, the service fails with a Failed to parse error. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

input_parameters.name: lenient
input_parameters.description: String Optional. A flag indicating whether Integration Server throws an exception if the inString value does not adhere to the format specified in currentPattern parameter. Set to: true to perform a lenient check. This is the default. In a lenient check, if the format of the date specified in the inString parameter does not match the format specified in the currentPattern parameter, Integration Server interprets and returns the date in the format specified in the currentPattern parameter. If the interpretation is incorrect, the service will return an invalid date. false to perform a strict check. In a strict check, the Integration Server throws an exception if the format of the date specified in the inString parameter does not match the format specified in the currentPattern parameter.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The date/time given by inString, in the format of newPattern.

usage_notes: As described in Notes on Invalid Dates, if the pattern yy is used for the year, dateTimeFormat uses a 50-year moving window to interpret the value of the year. If you need to change this behavior so that the year is interpreted as 80 years before or 20 years after the current date (as described in the Java class java.text.SimpleDateFormat), set the server configuration parameter watt.server.pubDateTimeFormat.javaSlidingWindow to true. For information about setting configuration parameters, see webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

By default, the Integration Server throws an exception if no input is passed to the service. To suppress the error message and return a null value for the value parameter, set the server configuration parameter watt.server.date.suppressPatternError to true. For information about setting configuration parameters, see webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .
If currentPattern does not contain a time zone, the value is assumed to be in the time zone of the webMethods Integration Server.
If newPattern contains a time zone, the time zone of the webMethods Integration Server is used.

services.name: pub.date:elapsedNanoTime
services.description: WmPublic. Calculates the time elapsed between the current time and the given time, in nanoseconds.

input_parameters.name: nanoTime
input_parameters.description: java.lang.Long Time in nanoseconds. If nanoTime is less than zero, then the service treats it as zero.

output_parameters.name: elapsedNanoTime
output_parameters.description: java.lang.Long The difference between the current time in nanoseconds and nanoTime. If nanoTime is greater than the current nano time, the service returns zero.

output_parameters.name: elapsedNanoTimeStr
output_parameters.description: String The difference between the current time in nanoseconds and nanoTime. The difference is expressed as a String, in this format: [years] [days] [hours] [minutes] [seconds] [millisec] [microsec] <nanosec> If nanoTime is greater than the current nano time, the service returns zero.

services.name: pub.date:formatDate
services.description: WmPublic. Formats a Date object as a string.

input_parameters.name: date
input_parameters.description: java.util.Date Optional. Date/time that you want to convert.

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern string that describes the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: timezone
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone, the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002, and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String The date/time given by date in the format specified by pattern.

services.name: pub.date:getCurrentDate
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the current date as a Date object.

services.name: pub.date:getCurrentDateString
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the current date as a String in a specified format.

input_parameters.name: pattern
input_parameters.description: String Pattern representing the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: timezone
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone, the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002, and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: value
output_parameters.description: String Current date in the format specified by pattern.

services.name: pub.date:getWorkingDays
services.description: WmPublic. Returns the number of working days between two dates.

input_parameters.name: startDate
input_parameters.description: String Starting date and time.

input_parameters.name: endDate
input_parameters.description: String Ending date and time.

input_parameters.name: startDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the startDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: endDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the endDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

output_parameters.name: workingDays
output_parameters.description: String Number of days between startDate and endDate excluding weekends and holidays.

usage_notes: The pub.date:getWorkingDays service requires you to configure holidays and weekend days in the holidays.cnf configuration file in Integration Server_directory \instances\instance_name\packages\WmPublic\config directory. The pub.date:getWorkingDays service uses this configuration file to find the number of working days between two dates.

services.name: pub.date:incrementDate

input_parameters.name: startDate
input_parameters.description: String Starting date and time.

input_parameters.name: startDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Format in which the startDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols.

input_parameters.name: endDatePattern
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Pattern representing the format in which you want the endDate to be returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols. If no endDatePattern is specified, the endDate will be returned in the format specified in the startDatePattern parameter.

input_parameters.name: addYears
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of years to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addMonths
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of months to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addDays
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of days to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addHours
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of hours to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addMinutes
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of minutes to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addSeconds
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of seconds to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: addMilliSeconds
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Number of milliseconds to add to startDate. The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

input_parameters.name: timezone
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Time zone in which you want the endDate to be expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

input_parameters.name: locale
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Locale in which the endDate is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002, and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server.

output_parameters.name: endDate
output_parameters.description: String The end date and time, calculated by incrementing the startDate with the specified years, months, days, hours, minutes, seconds, and/or milliseconds. The endDate will be in the endDatePattern format, if specified. If no endDatePattern is specified or if blank spaces are specified as the value, the endDate will be returned in the format specified in the startDatePattern parameter.

usage_notes: The addYears, addMonths, addDays, addHours, addMinutes, addSeconds, and addMilliSeconds input parameters can take positive or negative values. For example, If startDate is 10/10/2001, startDatePattern is MM/dd/yyyy, addYears is 1, and addMonths is -1, endDate will be 09/10/2002.

If you specify only the startDate, startDatePattern, and endDatePattern input parameters and do not specify any of the optional input parameters to increment the period, the pub.date:incrementDate service just converts the format of startDate from startDatePattern to endDatePattern and returns it as endDate.

Integration Server issues an exception if the format of the date specified in the startDate parameter does not match the format specified in the startDatePattern. Integration Server issues a similar exception if the format of the date specified in the endDate parameter does not match the endDatePatternformat.
